personal.name: Nikita Kazeev
personal.title: Research Scientist at the intersection of AI and Physics

titles.safety: Research Scientist focusing on Reliability and Uncertainty in Agentic Systems
titles.agents: Research Scientist focusing on AI Agents & ML Reliability

note.title: h-index
note.body: And from the height of my [citation pile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=vamy2okAAAAJ), I'm telling you: don't use h-index to judge people

differentiators.body: **Generative modelling for scientific data** — GANs, autoregressive transformers, and diffusion; from fast detector simulation to symmetry-aware crystal generation.
**Agent Reliability & UQ** — developed geometric and statistical frameworks to predict LLM agent reasoning failures using semantic abstraction trajectories.

**Aspirational Alignment & Verification** — formulated alignment strategies based on high-level human values and established benchmarks (e.g. Foresight-Phys) for validating AI systems in complex environments.
**Uncertainty quantification** — built the first methods for estimating the uncertainty of conditional GANs; calibrated generative and discriminative models.
**ML on structured, non-text data** — tabular, graph, and atomistic representations, spanning gradient boosting to transformers.
**Inference and systems at scale** — C++ production integration, large-scale DFT/VASP on HPC clusters, and reproducible experiment pipelines.
**Physics domain depth** — high-energy and condensed-matter physics; first-principles intuition that shapes model design.
**Research leadership** — lead multidisciplinary teams, chair conferences, and mentor students from idea to publication.

highlights.title: Leadership
bullets: Program Chair for the [AI4X 2025/6 conferences](https://ai4x.cc/)
Main organiser of the [ICLR 2025 Workshop on Machine Learning Multiscale Processes](https://multiscale-ai.github.io/)
Co-PI of a US$3.4m AI Singapore grant on multiscale machine learning

highlights.title: LLM Agent Safety & Alignment
bullets: Conceived and developed a novel geometric framework to predict multi-step reasoning failures in LLM agents using semantic abstraction trajectories
Designed uncertainty quantification (UQ) algorithms for LLM reasoning loops, improving agent error detection and calibration

Developed and open-sourced [reference-checker](https://github.com/kazeevn/referenece-checker), a tool for verifying claims and citation references in scientific papers to detect and mitigate LLM hallucinations
Authored a position paper on Pluralistic Alignment, advocating for aligning AI models with aspirational human values rather than replicating behavioral flaws

highlights.title: Wyckoff Transformer, a generative model for materials
bullets: Developed a coordinate-free permutation-invariant autoregressive Transformer encoder model for material generation and property prediction based on symmetry inductive bias
Increased materials discovery yield (S.U.N.) by 1.24x
Cut inference to 0.05 GPU-ms/structure — 4 orders of magnitude faster than prior models
Implemented the model in PyTorch; production-ready package published on [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/wyckoff-transformer/)
Orchestrated large-scale (10k structures) DFT computations with VASP on an HPC cluster
Led a team of 6

highlights.title: Wyckoff Transformer, a generative model for materials
bullets: Alignment by design: enforced physical principles directly at the architectural level as hard constraints, mathematically guaranteeing plausible generations
Solved a systemic failure mode where generative models default to asymmetric outputs despite symmetric training data, mitigating this structural bias via coordinate-free representations
Increased materials discovery yield (S.U.N.) by 1.24x while cutting inference time by 4 orders of magnitude (to 0.05 GPU-ms per structure)
Orchestrated large-scale (10k structures) DFT computation loops to verify out-of-distribution stability; led a team of 6

highlights.title: ML for defects in 2D crystals
bullets: Conceived a novel sparse representation of crystals with defects
Cut energy-prediction error 3.7× vs. the best prior model
Optimised training and inference: 4x less memory, 8x fewer GPU operations
Built a reproducible pipeline for parallel ML experiments
Led a team of 3

workExperience.note: [2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics](https://breakthroughprize.org/Laureates/1/L3995) as a member of LHCb.

highlights.title: Generative models uncertainty estimation
bullets: Developed the first methods for estimating uncertainty of conditional GANs
Led a team of 3 students

highlights.title: Generative models for fast simulation
bullets: Built a GAN for high-fidelity, tabular-data simulation of a Cherenkov detector
Reached ~10⁵× speed-up over the full Geant4 simulation

highlights.title: Machine learning on noisy data
bullets: Derived a statistically rigorous method to train classifiers on sPlot-weighted (label-noisy) data, standard in high-energy physics

highlights.title: Muon identification at the LHCb experiment at CERN
bullets: Solved a classification problem over tabular data with noisy labels under timing constraints
Built a CatBoost model that cut the false-positive rate ~30% in the critical low-momentum region
Shipped the model into the LHCb trigger (C++ & Python)
Packaged the work as a data science competition problem for IDAO-2019

highlights.title: CatBoost aka fighting biases with dynamic boosting
bullets: Built the team's distributed experiment infrastructure (Bash/Python)
Studied gradient boosting improvements with experiments on toy data

highlights.title: CatBoost: Combatting Biases in Boosting
bullets: Designed and built distributed experiment infrastructure to scale systematic bias analysis across large-scale tabular datasets
Investigated dynamic boosting formulations to eliminate target leakage and prediction shift, mitigating statistical bias in gradient boosting

education.title: HSE University & Sapienza Università di Roma
education.degree: PhD in Computer Science and Physics (Double Degree)
details: Thesis: [Machine Learning for particle identification in the LHCb detector](https://repository.cern/records/034yx-nt191)

education.title: Product Management course at Yandex
education.degree: Focused coursework in product strategy and execution.

education.title: Yandex School of Data Analysis
details: Algorithms, machine learning, deep learning, and distributed systems.

education.title: Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology

degrees.detail: Optimisation of data processing of the LHCb experiment.
Wave-packet molecular dynamics of electrons in nonideal plasma.

mentorship.body: Mentored 9 students ([1](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/219430466), [2](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/296294066), [3](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/219524130), [4](https://www.hse.ru/en/edu/vkr/470561417), [5](https://www.hse.ru/en/edu/vkr/470935224), [6](https://www.hse.ru/ma/datasci/students/diplomas/631565328), [7](https://www.hse.ru/ma/datasci/students/diplomas/631565422), [8](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/624890096), [9](https://www.hse.ru/ba/ami/students/diplomas/834351454)), 3 interns, and student workshop projects ([1](https://sochisirius.ru/news/3148), [2](https://web.archive.org/web/20220512040654/https://academy.yandex.ru/posts/vossozdat-arkhitekturu-bazy-dannykh-i-nauchit-golosovogo-pomoschnika-predskazyvat-namereniya-polzovatelya)).

teachingAndOutreach.body: Pitch research to the general public through major newspaper coverage ([1](https://www.kommersant.ru/doc/7832307), [2](https://www.kommersant.ru/doc/6122817)), blog posts ([1](https://ifim.nus.edu.sg/tailor-made-2d-materials-became-closer-with-the-new-machine-learning-algorithm-developed-at-nus/), [2](https://communities.springernature.com/posts/sparse-representation-for-machine-learning-the-properties-of-defects-in-2d-materials)), a [science slam](https://elementy.ru/events/435506/IV_Nauchnye_boi_NIU_VShE_na_Dne_Vyshki), interviews ([1](https://web.archive.org/web/20210518043835/https://academy.yandex.ru/posts/beresh-i-razbiraeshsya), [2](https://habr.com/en/company/yandex/blog/422761/)), and a public [talk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zLFZ_z-7-JI&feature=youtu.be&t=7779).

Taught at the Machine Learning in High Energy Physics Summer School in [2015](https://indico.cern.ch/event/439520/), [2016](https://indico.cern.ch/event/497368/), [2017](https://indico.cern.ch/event/613571/), [2018](https://indico.cern.ch/event/687473/), [2019](https://indico.cern.ch/event/768915/), [2020](https://indico.cern.ch/event/838377/), [2021](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1025052/), and [2023](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1229514/).

Lecturer in the [award-winning](https://www.hse.ru/en/news/edu/259378493.html) Coursera Advanced Machine Learning [  specialisation](https://web.archive.org/web/20220127183223/https://www.coursera.org/specializations/aml)
Taught and developed an introductory data analysis [course](https://we.hse.ru/en/) at HSE.

service.body: Reviewer for NeurIPS, RSC Advances, Machine Learning: Science and Technology, Digital Discovery
Peer Staff Supporter at NUS, serving as a first-line support for mental wellbeing.
